entry.title: #SITSP2024 - SAP Build Code - The future of development with generative AI
entry.content:
Palestra: Generative AI com SAP BTP

Palestrante: Dante

Biografia do Palestrante: Dante é arquiteto de soluções e Head de SAP BTP na consultoria Lab to Dev, onde também é cofundador da iniciativa educacional Lab to Learn. Com uma trajetória iniciada em tecnologias de mercado, Dante atua desde os primórdios do SAP BTP, acumulando experiência prática e didática na aplicação da plataforma em projetos diversos.

*** Todas as palestras SITSP 2024 ***

Descrição dos Assuntos Abordados:

  • Introdução ao SAP BTP: Explicação do que é a plataforma SAP BTP, seus cinco pilares (desenvolvimento de aplicações, automação, integração, dados & analytics e inteligência artificial).

  • Histórico da IA: Linha do tempo da evolução da inteligência artificial, desde os primeiros algoritmos if-else, passando por machine learning, deep learning e chegando à era da Generative AI.

  • Perigos da IA genérica: Riscos como alucinações e vazamento de dados em modelos como o ChatGPT foram discutidos, ressaltando a importância de soluções corporativas como a Julie.

  • Julie - CoPilot SAP: Apresentação da assistente Julie, uma AI integrada ao SAP BTP e SuccessFactors. Explicação de como ela utiliza modelos como GPT-4, LLaMA e IBM Watson, mantendo segurança e governança de dados.

  • SAP Build Code: Demonstração prática de como criar aplicações fullstack utilizando Build Code. A Julie foi usada para gerar modelos de dados, serviços OData e mock data com base em prompts simples.

  • Low code vs. Pro code: Diferenciação entre os modos de desenvolvimento disponíveis no BTP: Build Apps (low code), Process Automation, Work Zone (UX) e Build Code (pro code).

  • Exemplo prático: Desenvolvimento ao vivo de um aplicativo de seleção de almoço para participantes de evento, com geração automatizada de entidades, serviços e interface Fiori.

  • Tecnologias envolvidas: Discussão sobre CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model), uso de Node.js, JavaScript e TypeScript no BTP, e a presença do ABAP Cloud como caminho de transição para desenvolvedores tradicionais.

  • Encerramento: Convite para seguir os conteúdos semanais da Lab to Learn e aprofundar-se nos recursos do SAP BTP com a ajuda da Julie e outras ferramentas emergentes.

A palestra foi bem-humorada, repleta de insights técnicos e altamente recomendada para quem deseja entender o papel da inteligência artificial generativa no presente e futuro do ecossistema SAP.

entry.title: Re: #SITSP2024 - Desafios inovação no negócio e impactos para S/4HANA
entry.content: Excelente resumo da palestra. Obrigado!

entry.title: SAP Inside Track São Paulo 2024 para relembrar
entry.content:
Os temas abordados reforçou o compromisso do SITSP (Veja o blog do evento) com a evolução contínua da comunidade. Os participantes puderam mergulhar em temas como:

  • Desafios da inovação em negócios com impacto direto no S/4HANA, com reflexões profundas sobre os caminhos para uma adoção mais eficaz e estratégica da tecnologia.

  • Abordagens práticas de Material Ledger e Margin Analysis, que continuam a gerar dúvidas no universo do SAP CO.

  • Extensibilidade in-app no S/4HANA e SAP Build Code com IA generativa, temas que mostram o quanto o desenvolvimento está evoluindo com as novas ferramentas no ecossistema SAP.

  • Clean Core, Tax Engine, e integrações com cartões foram temas centrais para quem atua com arquitetura e compliance.

O evento contou com a presença de profissionais renomados do mercado, muitos dos quais são membros ativos da comunidade SAP, contribuindo constantemente com conteúdos, insights e mentorias.

Confira abaixo um resumo das palestras realizadas:

Palestras

Título da Palestra Descrição da Palestra Nome do Palestrante Link da Palestra

Desafios inovação no negócio e impactos para S/4HANAAnálise dos principais desafios para inovar dentro das empresas e como esses impactam a adoção e transformação com S/4HANA.@MRobalinho Link
Desafios do material ledger e Margin Analysis S/4HANADiscussão sobre os aspectos técnicos e funcionais das funcionalidades de contabilidade de custos no S/4HANA.@RogerioFaleiros Link
Arquitetura Orientada a Eventos com SAPExplorando como a orientação a eventos pode ser aplicada em integrações SAP modernas.Danilo OliveiraLink
S/4HANA In-App Extensibility para FuncionaisDemonstração prática das capacidades de extensibilidade nativa do S/4HANA para consultores funcionais.@Kessia Link
SAP Build Code - The future of development with generative AIVisão sobre o uso de IA generativa no desenvolvimento de soluções com SAP Build.Leandro DanteLink
Clean Core - o que é? Como atingir? BTP? Tachyonix?Abordagem estratégica e técnica sobre como manter o core limpo usando soluções da SAP e parceiros.Lucas FerreiraLink
Compreendendo Valores Planejados na Contabilidade de Custos no SAP S/4HANADiscussão sobre os conceitos de valores planejados e sua importância no controle gerencial.@Marssel700Link
Descomplicando o processo de recebimento de cartões integrando ao S/4HANAApresentação prática de integração de meios de pagamento com SAP.Luciano CasadoLink
SAP S/4HANA - TAXBRA/RVABRA versus External Tax EngineComparativo entre as soluções fiscais nativas da SAP e motores fiscais externos.Antonio TascinLink
Inteligência artificial aplicada para analytics e planejamento financeiroComo a IA pode transformar processos de planejamento e análises em empresas.Andrey MenegassiLink
Keep The Core Clean sem ficar Crazy - Uma visão Funcional + EncerramentoReflexão prática e estratégica sobre Clean Core com um olhar funcional, encerrando o evento.@Secco Link

Se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

Vídeos de edições anteriores (SP e CPS): 2025CPS 2024 2023 2017 2017CPS 2016 2016DEV < 2014

entry.title: Re: Meetup Fortaleza 2025
entry.content:
Ontem reunimos para validar o projeto da organização do SAP Inside Track Fortaleza 2025.

Pontos discutidos:

1) Orçamento;

2) Palestras propostas;

3) Confeção de blusas e aquisição de brindes;

4) Disponibilização de som, imagem e musica no hotel do evento;

5) Participação da organização em outros eventos SAP Inside Track no Brazil.

A conversa foi boa e bem participada. Aqui ficam alguma imagens do encontro.

entry.title: Re: SAP Screen Personas Script - Is it possible to fetch...
entry.content:
There is no separate standard Personas feature to consume a CDS view. However, in an on premise or private cloud system you can create an RFC-enabled function module to do this.

Since calling function modules is not supported in a public cloud system, this is currently not possible there.

entry.title: Warehouse Transfers
entry.content:
Hello,

I have 2 warehouses that are far away from each other. To transfer stock from one to another I have transport costs. 

Since transfer orders do not have landed costs, how can I have those costs inserted in product cost in the new warehouse? 

Tanks

Filipe

and there is my packaging specification

entry.title: Hello from Rio de Janeiro!
entry.content:
Hello! My name is Michelle and I have been a SAP consultant since 2007.

I have worked in several places around the world with SAP and I recently returned to work in my city, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. 

Last year, I started, together with other important consultants, to promote the SAP community in Brazil and currently, my goal is to lead the SAP community in Rio de Janeiro to receive better content and knowledge.

🙂

entry.title: The Delta Mechanism of 2LIS_02_ITM ?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Step 1.  There are 3 record of "MCEX02" in LBWQ .

Step 2.  Schedule Job immediately in LBWE,  and make sure no record in the "MCEX02".

Step 3.  Run the " Set delta status to 'Fetched' " of the DTP of the datasource 2LIS_02_ITM.

Step 4.  Create new 10 records in the  "MCEX02" and Schedule Job immediately again in LBWE, make sure the "MCEX02" is empty.

Step 5.  Run the Delta DTP of 2LIS_02_ITM.

But there is no data in the request.  Why ? Any step missing ?

Tks.

entry.title: Re: Using Association in SAP Datasphere

entry.title: Make yourself at Home

Dear SAP Business One community

Today I want to talk about how you could with a few clicks design your own home page view.

Let’s look at this video to learn more.

You can find additional information in the SAP Business One Web client user guide and the Learning journey.

Regards

Ari

entry.title: Best Approach to Trigger SAP CPI Interface Periodically from S/4HANA with Dynamic Plant/S.Loc
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement where a SAP CPI interface needs to be triggered once or twice daily. This interface performs the following:

--Accepts Plant and Storage Location as input.

--Fetches product data using an OData API in S/4HANA and some additional info from other APIs.

--Sends the final data to a target system.

What we initially considered:

Timer-based iFlow in CPI, with plant and storage location maintained as externalized parameters in a content modifier.

----Rejected by the team due to manual effort involved in changing parameters each time we want to trigger for different plant/storage location.

SAP BTP Job Scheduler: Allows periodic triggering and could call a CPI endpoint with required parameters. Currently in discussion. If selected, the job would pass Plant and Storage Location as part of the payload to CPI.

S/4HANA-triggered interface via SOAP/IDoc: S/4 can periodically send data (plant and storage location) via a custom ABAP job, which triggers a CPI endpoint using SOAP or sends an IDoc. This seems promising, but I would like to know what’s more standard/recommended.

Constraints:

We cannot use custom Z-tables in S/4HANA to manage or store configuration.

CPI as a public API for third-party to call isn’t suitable, as the source system must be S/4 or CI.

Event Mesh is not applicable here, since there is no real-time change or event-based trigger. It’s purely a periodic push.

My Questions:

1) Between BTP Job Scheduler and S/4HANA SOAP/IDoc push, which would be more recommended and scalable?

2) Are there any other better or more standard options to periodically trigger a CPI interface with dynamic parameters like plant and storage location?

entry.title: Re: SAC - group accounts in AccountLookup ( parent - child )

entry.content: So, as I said, no "check" needed.

entry.title: Re: BR role should only be available in access request
entry.content:
Hello everyone, @madhusap 

Not sure if this thread is still active.

I am facing the similar issue. We have a requirement where we are using business roles for particular landscape and other landscape is composite roles.

For business roles, We added composite role as associated role keeping the Role status as DEV/TEST for composite role and PRD for business role. But, When we submit the access request the audit log show No provisioning log is available. We can set some condition in access request search configuration to business role only since there are other landscape where requests will be submitted for composite roles.

Can you please suggest your ideas on how to solve this.

entry.title: Re: Can in-cell charts in SAC tables be enable/disabled u...
entry.content:
@VivekD 

I do not think there is an API to toggle on and off the in cell charts. If its really important for end user experience, You could superimpose 2 tables one with in cell charts enabled and other without it and then you can hide and unhide the table based on if in cell chart is required or not. But again only do this if its really a pressing requirement. as 2 tables will have an impact on performance.

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Wert zum Merkmal kann nicht gelöscht werden CS / Value for characteristic cannot be deleted CS
entry.content:
Hello all

We have the problem that in one configuration there are inexplicable messages like the one in the title. The characteristic value or the characteristic CS is not available.
This can occur with different characteristics and also regardless of whether this is a numeric field or a character field

Has someone an idea ?

BR Uli

entry.title: Re: Custom validation to prevent a promotion increase without actual job promotion
entry.content:
Hey @xavierlegarrec 

Quick question regarding custom validations in general – is it possible to configure them to trigger only at a specific step in the route map?

For example, managers enter their salary recommendations, and the next step is HR validation. We’d like the hard stop validations to be enforced only during the HR validation step, not during the manager input step.

Thank you

Regards,

entry.title: Implementing National ID with Validity Period in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
entry.content:
Hello All,

Greetings of the day! 

Welcome to New SAP Comminity Blog!

Most of the SuccessFactors customers are looking for this requirement - "Implementing National ID with Validity Period". In this blog post, I’m excited to share insights on the latest enhancement delivered in the 1H 2025 release related to the National ID Validity Period in SAP SuccessFactor Employee Central. This enhancement brings more flexibility and control in configuring National ID Types and Validity for different country specific - especially useful for compliance and data governance in HR systems.

New National ID Information with Validity Period - General Availability

You can now configure the validity periods for each national ID record to track lifelong changes to your national ID information. To support tracking lifelong changes to your national ID information, we have added the validity fields Valid From and Valid To for each National ID records in National ID Information.

The National ID with Validity Period feature allows you to have multiple national ID records under one card type or across multiple card types.

  • New validity fields Valid From and Valid To in National ID Information
  • New fields record-id, valid-from, and valid-to to the nationalIdCard HRIS element in Business Configuration UI (BCUI)

Configuration Requirements:

  • To use the National ID with Validity Period feature, you need to add and enable the new HRIS fields record-id, valid-from, and valid-to to the nationalIdCard HRIS element in Business Configuration UI (BCUI).
  • Once the new fields are enabled in BCUI, the existing OData v2 entity PerNationalId becomes unavailable and you need to use the new entity PerNationalIdWithValidityPeriod to access national ID information. If you are currently using PerNationalId for integrations and extensions, please transition to using PerNationalIdWithValidityPeriod.

Enabling Fields in BCUI for National ID with Validity Period

To use the National ID with Validity Period feature, you need to configure valid-from, valid-to, and record-id in BCUI.

Prerequisites:

  • You have the view and edit permission under User Permissions > Employee Data > HR Information > Record ID.

Step – by – Step Configuration Procedure for BCUI Fields:

  1. Go to Manage Business Configuration from Admin Center.
  2. Expand Employee Central > HRIS Elements, and select nationalIdCard.
  3. In the HRIS fields section, click the dropdown of any textbox, and locate record-id.
  4. Select Yes from the Enabled dropdown.
  5. Repeat step 3 and 4 for configuring valid-from and valid-to.
  6. Choose Save.

Configuring National ID Allowlist for Multi-record Relationship

Use the Manage Data tool to configure the multi-record relationship for the specified country/region. You can define how multiple national ID records are validated, for example, multiple records under one card type or sequential records across card types.

Prerequisites Role-Based Permissions:

  • You have enabled the permission under Administrator Permissions > Metadata Framework > Manage Data.

  • View and edit permission under User Permissions > Employee Data > HR Information > Record ID

  • You have enabled the view and edit permissions under User Permissions > Miscellaneous Permissions > Allowlist Configuration for National ID with Validity Period.
  • You have enabled the view and edit permissions under User Permissions > Miscellaneous Permissions > Allowlist Configuration Details for National ID with Validity Period.

Context:

If no allowlist configuration is done for a specific national ID card type, multiple records related to this card type will not be allowed.

In the case of changes in gender or other country/region-specific identification information, you need to renew your National ID number. Then, you can configure an allowlist to update multiple national ID records under one national ID card type. There should be no gaps or overlaps in the validity periods of these multiple national ID records.

In some countries/regions, you can have multiple national ID card types whose validity periods must follow a specific sequence based on the legal requirements. In this case, you can configure an allowlist to update sequential national ID records across different national ID card types. There should be no gaps or overlaps in the validity periods of these multiple national ID records.

  • A new Allowlist Configuration Details for National ID with Validity Period MDF object in Manage Data:

Use the object to configure the multi-record relationship for the specified country/region. You can define how multiple National ID records are validated, for example, multiple records under one card type or sequential records across card types. If no allowlist configuration is done for a specific national ID card type, multiple records related to this card type will not be allowed.

  • Validation checks in case of one or multiple National ID records in National ID Information and Import Employee Data (purge type Full Purge and Incremental Upload) page
  • New OData v2 entity PerNationalIdWithValidityPeriod: The new entity replaces the existing PerNationalId entity when record-id, valid-from, and valid-to are enabled in BCUI. It uses personIdExternal and recordId as business keys and includes two new properties validFrom and validTo, allowing you to query and upsert national ID information that contains specific validity periods.

Go to Configure Object Definition:

Allowlist Configuration for National ID with Validity Period (NationalIdWithValidityPeriodAllowList) - Parent Object Name

This parent object is used to manage multiple child records of national identification (like CNIC, Passport, etc.) with different validity periods under Multi or Sequential card configurations.

Association to Child Object:

  • You have a One-to-Many Multiplicity.
  • Destination Object: Allowlist Configuration Details for National ID with Validity Period (Child Object)
  • Association Name: toNationalIdWithValidityPeriodAllowListDetails
  • Type: Composite (Association Type)
  • This purpose of this Parent to Child association is to maintain multiple national ID records in the child object.
NameMultiplicityDestination ObjectAssociation Type
toNationalIdWithValidityPeriodAllowListDetails
One To Many
Allowlist Configuration Details for National ID with Validity Period
Composite

Allowlist Configuration Details for National ID with Validity Period (NationalIdWithValidityPeriodAllowListDetails) - Child Object Name

This child object is used to define detailed allowlist settings for National ID validity periods. It inherits effective dating from the parent and includes key fields like country, sequential type, and card types

Purpose of the Parent Object Designed:
  • Configure how various National ID card types (like CNIC, Passport, RP Card, etc.).
  • Define which card types are allowed for an employee, including validity period handling.
  • Support Multi-record (same card type, different periods) and Sequential (different card types over time) structures.

Step – by – Step Configuration Procedure for AllowList Configuration object:

  1. Go to Admin Center > Manage Data.
  2. From the Create New dropdown, select Allowlist Configuration for National ID with Validity Period.
  3. Specify the country/region.
  4. Specify the Multiple Records under One Card Type from the Multi-record Relationship and choose Details.
  5. Enter the details in the fields and choose Done.
  6. Choose Save to exit allowlist configuration.
  7. Enter the details in the fields and choose done.
  8. Choose Save.

Allowlist Configuration for National ID with Validity Period.

Field

Entry

Country/Region

The form autopopulates with your preconfigured values.

Multi-record Relationship

National ID Card Type

Specify a national ID card type under which multiple national ID records are allowed and validated.

Allowlist Configuration Details Multiple Records under One Card Type from the Multi-record Relationship for National ID with Validity Period.

  1. Go to Admin Center > Manage Data.
  2. From the Create New dropdown, select Allowlist Configuration for National ID with Validity Period.
  3. Specify the country/region.
  4. Specify the Sequential Records under One Card Type from the Multi-record Relationship and choose Details.
  5. Enter the details in the fields and choose Done.
  6. Choose Save to exit allowlist configuration.
  7. Enter the details in the fields and choose done.
  8. Choose Save.

Field

Entry

Country/Region

The form autopopulates with your preconfigured values.

Multi-record Relationship

National ID Card Type (First)

Specify the national ID card types (3 at maximum) so that you can have multiple national ID records in a sequence across different card types. The sequence numbers (first, second, and third) and defined card types will help validate your national ID records.

National ID Card Type (Second)

National ID Card Type (Third)

Allowlist Configuration Details Sequential Records under One Card Type from the Multi-record Relationship for National ID with Validity Period.

Multi-Record VS Sequential Record Configurations:

Now understand the Core Concept of National ID with Validity Period - for Multi-Record and Sequential Record Configurations in SAP SuccessFactors for Allowlist configuration Detials

Multi-Records under One Card Type:

In the Multi Record Card Type, we have to mark only one card type as Primary = Yes. Multi Record Card Type is defined with two categorizations. For example, the validity period of CNIC may change over a specific time period, but its number will never change – it remains the same for a lifetime – so it will be Primary = Yes.

For another card type like Passport, it will be Primary = No, because Multi Record Card Type supports only one card type as Primary = Yes.

Now, the primary one is the one whose card number does not change, but its validity period changes – we place such records under Multi in Manage Data → Allowlist Configuration.
Passport will also fall under multi card type by nature.

  • Primary = Yes → the one whose card number does not change (e.g., CNIC)
  • Primary = No → Card number may change (e.g., Passport)
Overlapping Example (Would Be Rejected):

Record

Valid From

Valid To

CNIC-001

01-Jan-2024

31-Dec-2025

CNIC-002

01-Dec-2025

15-Aug-2028

For Multi Record Card Type in one category, just keep in mind:
  • No gaps are allowed in validity periods
  • One record, such as National ID card number, must be Primary
  • Other records, like Passport, do not have to be Primary
  • You can only have one primary, all other records must be Primary = No
  • If more than one record is marked as primary, the system will throw an error
  • Note: National ID Information 1234567893 Please set the same “Is Primary” value for the national ID card type Civil Number in the country/region Oman.

Sequential Records Across Card Types:

This configuration supports different card types in a sequence, such as when an employee transitions between countries or legal statuses — for example, moving from using a Passport to a Resident ID.

Consider a student currently residing in Pakistan who holds a CNIC (Computerized National Identity Card). This CNIC is valid only within Pakistan.

As the student moves to Austria for higher education, their Passport becomes the valid identity document for international travel and identification, particularly for Austria.

Later, upon receiving their Resident Permit (RP) card in Austria, the RP card becomes the valid identification for residency within Europe.

Overlapping Example (Would Be Rejected):

Record

Valid From

Valid To

CNIC-001

01-Jan-2024

31-Dec-2025

CNIC-002

01-Dec-2025

15-Aug-2028

For Sequential Record Card Type in one category, just keep in mind:
  • The identity records transition sequentially across different card types (CNIC → Passport → RP Card).
  • Each of these cards is marked as Primary = Yes even if earlier ones become invalid, because they reflect historical and legal identity validity across time and regions.
  • There should be no gaps between the validity periods of these records.
  • All records in a sequential relationship must be marked as Primary, otherwise the system will throw a validation error.
  • Now in the system, the validity of the previous card will end, and a new card will appear.
    The new card will exist, but the old one will still be marked as Primary = Yes, even if its validity has expired, because its existence will still remain in the system.
  • Note: National ID Information 507080-4870993 Please set the same “Is Primary” value for the national ID card type FRN - Foreign Registration Number, Korea Registration Number, RGN - Resident Registration Number in the country/region Korea, Republic of.

What's Changed

How It Looks Now

National ID with Validity Period

Allowlist Configuration Sample for National ID with Validity Period

How It Looked Before

National ID Information

Technical Details:

Reference Number

ECT-242645

Product 

  • Employee Central

Module 

  • Localization
  • Employee Data
  • Integration and Extension

Feature

  • Country/Region Specifics
  • Business Configuration
  • API

Action 

Info only

Enablement 

Customer configured

Link to Demo

 

Lifecycle 

General Availability

Type

New

Major or Minor

Major

Business Process Variant

  • Hire to Retire

Software Version 

  • 1H 2025

Valid as Of 

2025-05-16

Latest Revision 

2025-04-04

Regards,

Mahnoor Naseem

SAP SuccessFactors Consultant

If you found this blog a useful please consider giving it a Kudos

entry.title: Re: Lookup Table Business Rule
entry.content:
Hello,

Please share the Screenshots again unable to see what changes you have made to business rule.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP b1 10.0 Hana Fiori Cockpit Icon Change Issue

I'm very new to python, I hvae a data set with 13 column , once of the called 'gender'. I want to filter the data and result moved to Target table.

However I'm getting below error message called "'method' object is not subscriptable"

Please my code below, unfortunatly we can't debug the code in datasphere

def transform(data):
specific_name = ['Male']
df = data['gender'].isin[('specific_name')]
#data[NV]
return df

Any input would be greatly appreciated

Kind regards

Hari

entry.title: Re: Migration data to S/4HANA on-premise
entry.content: Is it possible to extracting data from S/4 HANA Rise to Oracle data base by BODS. If yes please let me know how?

entry.title: Re: Lookup Table Business Rule
entry.content: It sounds like the issue might be related to how the lookup table is being queried or how the matching logic is set up in your rule. If the rule is only picking the first entry in the table regardless of the actual values in the requisition, a few things could be causing that: * **Lookup condition might be too broad or missing a key match**, so it's defaulting to the top row. Double-check that both *Country* and *Grade* are being passed exactly as they appear in the lookup table (case sensitivity, spaces, etc.). * **Rule logic priority** – sometimes, when using multiple conditions in a business rule, the engine can return an early match if the rule isn't structured carefully. Try reordering or explicitly nesting your logic. * **Test with mock data** – manually test the same values that are in the lookup table to confirm whether they match when hardcoded. This helps verify if the rule or the data itself is off. If neither version of your rule works as expected, consider reaching out through SAP support or a consultant to help you validate whether this is a configuration quirk or a bug in how the rule evaluates lookup results.

entry.title: Part Period Balancing in PP/DS (SAP_PP_005 Heuristic)
entry.content:
In order to collect requirements and form a greater lot of procurement, the conventional way is to use periodical lot sizes. Built in lot sizes that SAP offers are; for instance, TB (daily), WB (weekly), MB (monthly), etc.

On the other hand; SAP offers optimum lot sizing procedures, as well as periodical lot sizes.

 

In PP/DS; optimum lot sizing is not handled by Lot Sizing Procedure field in MRP1 view of material master, but by dedicated product heuristics. These heuristics are:

  • SAP_PP_005 Part Period Balancing
  • SAP_PP_006 Least-Unit Cost Proc.: Ext. Procurement
  • SAP_PP_013 Groff Procedure

In usage of any of these 3 product heuristics, Lot Sizing Procedure can be left as EX (Lot-for-lot order quantity), SAP still takes care of lot sizing of procurement plans by combining requirement quantities.

 

In this blog we are focusing on SAP_PP_005 heuristic, which is Part Period Balancing.

Part Period Balancing heuristic is based on 2 costs maintained on Advanced Planning view of material master:

  • Procurement Costs
  • Production Storage Costs

In this example; stock cost is set to 5, which is per unit per day; and procurement cost is set to 100, which is fixed cost of additional order.

 

Let's check some requirements:

  • 01.07.2025 FC req. 10-
  • 15.07.2025 FC req. 10-
  • 01.08.2025 FC req. 10-
  • 15.08.2025 FC req. 10-
  • 01.09.2025 FC req. 10-
  • 15.09.2025 FC req. 10-

 

Let's run product heuristic:

  • 01.07.2025 PurRqs 30
  • 01.07.2025 FC req. 10-
  • 15.07.2025 FC req. 10-
  • 01.08.2025 FC req. 10-
  • 15.08.2025 PurRqs 30
  • 15.08.2025 FC req. 10-
  • 01.09.2025 FC req. 10-
  • 15.09.2025 FC req. 10-

As seen, first 3 requirements are combined.

 

Why? Let's see how the algorithm works step by step:

  1. First, the algorithm runs net requirements calculation and finds open requirements by covering by stocks and fixed receipts.
  2. Then, the remaining open requirements are listed chronologically. In our example all requirements are open, because there is no stock or fixed receipt. So, first open requirement is on 01.07.2025 with quantity of 10, and second requirement is on 15.07.2025 with quantity of 10.
  3. Algorithm works iteratively. So, it first check whether first 2 requirements need to be combined or not. If combined, there will be 20 units of receipt to create, 10 of which will stay at stock for 14 days. Which means 14 days * 10 units * 5 stock cost per unit per day = 700. Since the receipt will be 20, average daily stock cost will be 700 / 20 = 35. Since 35 is lower than the procurement cost of 100 (fixed cost of an additional order); having separate orders is worse, and having 1 order covering these 2 requirements is better.
  4. Now the algorithm knows that first 2 requirements need to be combined. But it does not stop there, it iterates further. Now, it investigates whether combining 3 requirements would be better or not.
  5. If first 3 requirements are combined, there will be 30 units of receipt to create, 10 of which will stay at stock for 14 days, and 10 of which will stay at stock for 31 days. Which means 14 days * 10 units * 5 stock cost per unit per day + 31 days * 10 units * 5 stock cost per unit per day = 700 + 1550 = 2250. Since the receipt will be 30, average daily stock cost will be 2250 / 30 = 75. Since 75 is lower than the procurement cost of 100 (fixed cost of an additional order); having separate order for 3rd requirement is worse, and having 1 order covering these 3 requirements is better.
  6. Now the algorithm knows that first 3 requirements need to be combined. But it does not stop there, it iterates further. Now, it investigates whether combining 4 requirements would be better or not.
  7. If first 4 requirements are combined, there will be 40 units of receipt to create, 10 of which will stay at stock for 14 days, 10 of which will stay at stock for 31 days, and 10 of which will stay at stock for 45 days. Which means 14 days * 10 units * 5 stock cost per unit per day + 31 days * 10 units * 5 stock cost per unit per day + 45 days * 10 units * 5 stock cost per unit per day = 700 + 1550 + 2250 = 4500. Since the receipt will be 40, average daily stock cost will be 4500 / 40 = 112,5. Since 112,5 is higher than the procurement cost of 100 (fixed cost of an additional order); having separate order for 4th requirement is better. The deal is closed. It is decided to combine first 3 requirements.
  8. The same iteration starts for the rest of requirements. Now first open requirement is on 15.08.2025 with quantity of 10. And combination check is carried out again and again until there is no requirement left.

 

Tips & Tricks

For externally procured materials stock cost and procurement cost can be defined as in real life. However; for in-house produced materials, it may be unclear to determine procurement cost.

Personally, for in-house materials, I suggest defining stock cost as 1, and procurement cost as "maximum days' supply per unit".

So, let's say we have a semi-finished good, and we want to combine requirement quantities and produce it in bigger lots; but we do not want to define a certain period to create a lot (as in weekly, for instance). Because we want to form a lot of semi-finished good based on requirement quantities and dates. In this case, we can use Part Period Balancing for the semi-finished good, by setting maximum days' supply per unit as 15 days, for instance. Therefore, stock cost will be 1 and procurement cost will be 15.

One more thing; let's say the base unit of measure is too granular to model, for example, it is process industry and production is carried out in tons, but the base unit of measure is kilogram, stock cost can be set as 0,001 so that it will still be 1 for a ton.

This offering is not available or may not be part of an edition of SAP Learning Hub that you have access to. Please click here to download an overview of all current content per edition.

Ruchi

entry.title: Re: Logon sicf
entry.content:
I am not sure what "the URL link automatically enters" means. I don't know about anything about the service.

In general, the service consumer/client must authenticate to use the service. 
See https://help.sap.com/doc/saphelp_em92/9.2/en-US/48/c7c062da5e31ebe10000000a42189b/frameset.htm for login options to sicf.

Best via X.509. Do not use anonymous login. There might be exceptions. It is not relevant if you are on a dev test or prod system. You never know the options to jump from dev to prod.

For help on security, check:
SEC-374 - Use mTLS for secure system-to-system communication
SEC-230 - Provide standard identity management, authentication and single sign-on mechanisms
SEC-232 - Provide secure X.509 certificate authentication
SEC-309 - Manage keys, tokens, and passwords securely
I recommend to coordinate with your security expert.

entry.title: Extend CDS View , Redefine ODATA and Enhance BOBF Class to add custom fields in Standard Fiori apps.
entry.content:
How to Extend CDS View with Metadata Extension , Redefine ODATA service and Enhance BOBF class to add custom fields to standard Fiori App in SAP GTS.

Explain with step by step process using any example.

 

SAP Global Trade Services  OData  ABAP Development 

@SAP @sapcommunity @GTS @ABAP

entry.title: Re: Transaction - /SCWM/RFUI - To load second screen of t...
entry.content:
Was meinst du mit zweitem Bildschirm des Hauptmenüs? Kannst du da eventuell ein Beispiel Screenshotten?
Wenn du die Anmeldemaske meinst mit der Lagernummer, Ressource und Gerät, dass diese übersprungen wird, dann kannst du SAP Screen Personas benutzen und die Felder individuell füllen lassen.

--> Doppelklick auf das Feld und das Feld mit Daten füttern:

Zweite Antwort:

/SCWM/USER die Felder für den Nutzer jeweils pflegen, dann wird der Nutzer automatisch angemeldet.

 


Somit springt man direkt auf den Bildschirm des Hauptemenüs.

entry.title: Experience with SAP C4C V1 to Sales & Service Cloud V2 Migration – Transition Tools Feedback?
entry.content:
Hi every one, 

Has anyone in the SAP CX community carried out a migration from SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) Version 1 to SAP Sales & Service Cloud Version 2?

I'm about to take part in such a migration project and I'm particularly interested in your experience with the official Transition Tools — especially the Readiness Check and the Data Transfer Tool.

In theory, the Readiness Check helps identify which business objects are supported, partially supported, or not supported at all. However, I haven’t yet gone through the complete Data Transfer Tool process, and I’d love to hear real-life feedback:

Did you use it?

What was your experience like?

Any limitations or issues you encountered?

What should I be aware of before diving in? 

Thanks in advance for sharing your insights!

entry.title: Re: Compare to last year in numeric point
entry.content:
Hi there, 

I´m facing the same issue, and found that whenever you define a date range, SAC takes several dates values: i.e. "Q1 2024, Q2 2024" but the numeric point widget is only expecting a single date value: i.e. "Q1 2024", which is the "single value filter" required by the numeric point widget. 

SAC helps determines that for Numeric Point widgets, "when the variance uses a Time Range filter, the range must be set to only one interval (year, quarter, or period)"

Here the link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/876dca7992174148bb759c278abba463.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Admin. users of IAS through IAG or GRC access request

Hi All,

can Administrator users of type 'User' and 'System' be created from GRC(using GRC-IAG bridge) or through IAG access requests

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Re: no wm relevant components exist function not possible
entry.content: It helped thank you

entry.title: Re: Stay certified exam error > SAP Activate
entry.content: Can you access via the manage certification button when you go to the plane that shows your certs?

entry.title: Re: Stay certified exam error > SAP Activate
entry.content: The page not the plane

entry.title: Re: How to hide message in app Clear G/L accounts
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for sharing 

The error “Account 720100000 requires an account assignment” will be resolved by maintaining the default cost centre/profit centre in the “Manage Default Account Assignments” app.

The messages “Profitability segment being derived again” and “Profit centre was set...” are standard informational logs from CO-PA and cannot be hidden in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

I recommend you ensure that default account assignments are complete for all cost-relevant G/L accounts. Users should ignore the informational messages, as they do not impede postings

Let me know if you need further information

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Need Technical Help on how to do GR posting of Inbound Delivery in SAP EWM
entry.content:
I am having the requirement to develop a custom transaction having most of the functionalities copied from standard /scwm/gr transaction.

I am able to achieve till packing of quantity to HU (handling Unit) after HU's creation.

Now, I am stuck in posting of GR functionality of inbound delivery (already created). I debugged the entire standard /scwm/gr transaction and found several standard classes and methods which are being called for the same purpose but unable to use any as the methods are private. The class I found is /SCWM/CL_GR_VIEWER_IBD and the method is Post goods receipt.

I have checked the abap stack while debugging and found other classes too which are being further called i.e. /SCWM/CL_GR_SP_BASE, /SCWM/GR_VIEWER_BASE but again unable to use the methods in my custom development as either the class is abstract class or methods are not public.

Please help and guide technically on how to post the GR in EWM (Considering delivery is available, PO number is available, HU's data is available).

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: SAP SAC Data Action/Scripting - create new member and copy measures from one row to another
entry.content:
Hi All,

In a SAC planning model, l want to first choose a contract , then create a new member based on this contract for a dimension(planning number), and then copy all measures value of this contract to the rows, with contract and planning number.

Example:

Before duplication:

Contract NumberPlanning NumberPosition VersionDate PriceQuantity
2025-1Actual2025-04-011001000
2025-2Actual2025-05-012002000

After clicking the button:

Contract NumberPlanning NumberPosition VersionDate PriceQuantity
2025-1Actual2025-04-011001000
2025-2Actual2025-05-012002000
20252025_0011Planning2025-04-011001000
20252025_0012Planning2025-05-012002000

Goal:
When a user select a contract and then clicks a **button**, I want to:
1. Copy all rows for the selected contract (`Contract Number`).
2. Generate a new `Planning Number` using the format: `ContractNumber_0001`, incrementing for each new copy.
3. Set the `Version` to "Planung".
4. Add these new rows back into the dataset.

❓ Questions:
1. How can I implement this row duplication logic in SAC?
2. Can this be done using Data Actions? Or what advanced script should I use?
3. Is there an approach using scripting? But unbooked data can not use setUserinput(), when l show unbooked data, then there is performance problem because of too much unbooked date.

Any help or example code would be greatly appreciated!

l saw this article (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-sac-data-action-copy-measure-from-one-row-to-another/qaq-p/12710497)before, but it did not add new member and copy measures on this newly inserted row.

@N1kh1l 

Thank you,

Iker

# SAP Analytics Cloud

#SAP Analytics Cloud for planning

entry.title: Re: Part Period Balancing in PP/DS (SAP_PP_005 Heuristic)
entry.content: Great post! Well written and very informative, thank you for sharing!

entry.title: Re: What is the reason to provide profit center for bank...
entry.content:
Hello @nithin_pranesh 

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, profit center are required for all postings, including balance sheet accounts like bank accounts, due to the universal journal (ACDOCA) structure, which mandates complete financial dimension reporting.

Assigning a profit center enables accurate segment reporting, internal controls, and ensures compliance with financial reporting standards. While bank accounts are balance sheet accounts, the system still enforces profit center derivation to maintain consistency across all postings.

Best regards,

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Part Period Balancing in PP/DS (SAP_PP_005 Heuristic)
entry.content: Very informative and nice post , includes critical points and details. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Error message "No space left on device" during build of CAP application
entry.content: @anshuman_s22Do you deploy with mbt? If so, you can check results of the `mbt build` step, that makes the .mtar file in 7-Zip for example. There you can easily check if you accidently added a node_modules folder

entry.title: ABAP - Simple Transformation : Deserialization and optional tags ?
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm working on an XML transformation (STRANS - Simple Transformation type).
I'm having a few problems with a case that seemed simple to me ...

My XML is (simplified version, because the initial file can contain thousands of lines) :

<Invoice>
<ID>FB25-05-028</ID>
<IssueDate>2025-05-28</IssueDate>
<DueDate>2025-06-27</DueDate>
<InvoiceTypeCode listID="UNCL1001">380</InvoiceTypeCode>
<DocumentCurrencyCode listID="ISO4217">EUR</DocumentCurrencyCode>
<BillingReference>
<InvoiceDocumentReference>
<ID>TEST123</ID>
</InvoiceDocumentReference>
</BillingReference>
...
</Invoice>

For example, I just need to retrieve the <ID>, the <IssueDate> and the <ID> in <BillingReference>.
No problem with that.

The problem is that in some cases the <BillingReference> tag is not present, it's [0..1].
So her children are not always present.

How do you indicate in the ST that this tag <BillingReference> is optional?

My ST looks like :

<?sap.transform simple?>
<tt:transform xmlns:tt="http://www.sap.com/transformation-templates">

  <tt:root name="INVOICE"/>

  <tt:template>
    <Invoice tt:extensible="deep-dynamic">

      <ID tt:value-ref="INVOICE.ID"/>
	  <IssueDate tt:value-ref="INVOICE.ISSUEDATE"/>

      <tt:d-cond>
        <BillingReference>
          <InvoiceDocumentReference>
            <ID tt:value-ref="INVOICE.BILLINGREFERENCE_ID"/>
          </InvoiceDocumentReference>
        </BillingReference>
      </tt:d-cond>

    </Invoice>
  </tt:template>

</tt:transform>

With this version, I never go into <BillingReference>.

I tried using a d-cond like this: 

<tt:d-cond check="exist(INVOICE.BILLINGREFERENCE_ID)">

I've tried several solutions but nothing has worked.

My example is intentionally simplified.
I'm going to come across this kind of problem several times in the ST.

My XML sometimes has thousands of <tags>, so starting to list all the <tags> in the ST using SKIP statement isn't an option.

I hope I've just missed the simple solution to this problem.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content:
Hi everyone !

I’m new to this community, and I’d love to share a bit of my story and what brings me here.

My journey with SAP started back in 2013, when I was part of the purchasing team at an electronics manufacturing contractor (JABIL, operating in Ukraine). We were using the ERP MM module. I was always curious about the tech side of the business and wanted to understand how everything worked behind the scenes.

What really sparked my interest was a team member, an SAP Subject Matter Expert (SME). He had a superpower ability to answer any question about the ERP and solve any trouble with the system. It was like magic to me.

For various reasons, I wasn’t able to follow that path of inspiration, but it was a deep level of inception that happened at the time.

Fast forward to today, I’ve recently experienced a career change. I consider myself a software developer. Now, I finally feel that I have the technical foundation to truly dive into SAP and grow into an expert myself.

To be honest, I feel a bit of shame for not discovering the SAP resources early enough. It’s such a rich place. All the tutorials, insights, and this community… I feel inspired again. I’m grateful to be a part of it.

Thank you to everyone who contributes to this community and makes learning SAP such an exciting and accessible journey. I’m happy to be here !

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Data Action/Scripting - create new member and...
entry.content:
@iksuuu 

This requirement has multiple layers to it and its very difficult to provide an exact working code. I am providing some snippets from some similar requirements which you can adjust as per your requirement. Try to break this into parts.

  • Reading the selected contract number from table
  • Creating the planning number master data in the dimension
  • Assigning this member to a Data Action parameter
  • Execute the Data Action

 

Also I am assuming the Data Action would be already ready as its simply copying from planning number # to newly created one.

I do not have a logic of incrementing the counter every time but you could try reading all members of the dimensions planning number and see what is the last member sequence. Or you could have an attribute called sequence in the dimension and assign sequence number to it while creating the member.

Below is some form of rough scripting. I have not validated it for syntax as I took it from my old work and stitch it together so some syntax might be off.

//Reading the current  contract number members from  Table row selection
var sel =Table_1.getSelections();

	if (sel.length > 0) {
		var ContractmemberId = ArrayUtils.create(Type.string);
		
	for(var i=0;i<=sel.length;i++)
		{
	for (var dimensionId in sel[i]) {
 		
			
 		if (dimensionId === 'CONTRACT_NUMBER') {
			
 			var currentcontract = sel[i][dimensionId];

			if(ContractmemberId.indexOf(currentproduct) === -1 && currentcontract)
			{
			   ContractmemberId.push(currentcontract);
			
			}}
				
	

	}}}

//Create a script variable  MemberId of type planning member before the below

//Creating Planning number member

var planningnumberid=ContractmemberId+'_001' // think about how to increment the counter here


MemberId = ({id: "planningnumberid", description: "planningnumberid"); // you can change description

var result = PlanningModel_1.createMembers('PLANNING_NUMBER',MemberId);
if(result)
{
Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success,"Member Successfully Created");

// Assigning the parameter to Data Action
DataAction_1.setParameterValue("P_Planningnumber",planningnumberid); // You need to have a parameter in your Data Action for planning number



Application.showBusyIndicator("Preparing for execution..."); 
DataAction_1.execute();
Application.refreshData();
Application.hideBusyIndicator();
Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success,"Executed Successfully");


}
else
{
Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Error,'Failed to create Member');
						
}

 

Hope this will help !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Best Approach to Trigger SAP CPI Interface Periodical...
entry.content:
Hi

You can easily create a job in CPI that processes all plants one by one and then check for differences. 

Configuraiton for plant can happen via a value mapping

You can also add a web api to this to trigger processing of one plant if it needs to be run. 

Daniel

entry.title: Re: Time Tracking: Overtime should be calculated only for Approved Overtime entry
entry.content: Also have the same scenario, did you find anything ?

entry.title: Production Planning Optimization (PPO) - Part I
entry.content:
Introduction

In PP/DS we have been introduced with a Production Planning Optimization (PPO) functionality for a while. In this blog we will try to understand what PPO is, and step by step proceed until we feel comfortable to implement it.

 

Spoiler
Actually, people who worked with APO-SNP in the past, or currently working with IBP-S&OP or IBP-R&S are familiar with this concept.

 

We will start with defining and addressing it, then investigating the nature of optimization, then emphasizing restrictions, then modeling, and finally testing.

What is PPO?

PPO is a planning tool; that can consider component availability and resource capacities at once, for all relevant bill of material (BOM) levels at once, trying to fulfill any requirement on-time, as well as determining stock keeping materials and locations; resulting in consistent and feasible production/distribution/procurement plan.

Difference from Material Requirements Planning (MRP)

Constrained Planning

PPO is a constrained planning tool. Constraints may be due to lead times or capacities. Therefore, some requirements may not be fulfilled.

On the other hand, MRP always fulfills requirements.

Bucket-Oriented Planning

In order to compare available capacities with capacity requirements, PPO uses buckets. So unlike MRP or other PP/DS tools; it does not work in a time-continuous manner, but in defined buckets.

PDS Selection

Since PPO takes production capacities into account, when at least one of the finite resources of a PDS for a product is %100 loaded, it can switch to other PDS with available capacity. If PPO cannot find any PDS with available capacity within the required bucket, it tries for earlier buckets, if it still fails then later buckets.

On the other hand, MRP always select the preferred (most prior or first) PDS as long as it is valid (in terms of date and lot size) and as long as its lead time is small enough to cover requirement date, always on-time.

Component Availability

If a component cannot be planned accordingly, then an upstream receipt is not created either.

For instance; if there is a requirement on a finished good, and free capacity for it; but no capacity for semi-finished good or no time to deliver raw material; then no order is created for finished good as well.

On the other hand; in MRP, regardless from component and/or resource availability, upstream receipts are always created. A planner must evaluate MRP results and take action accordingly based on exceptions (e.g. delays) afterwards.

Priority Logic

Conventionally "prior" means "first" in PP/DS. But that is not the case for PPO. In PPO, "prior" means "last to move from bucket" or "last to be unfulfilled".

When a bucket has overloaded capacity and some orders need to move from the required bucket, prior materials are last to move. PPO tries to fulfill the requirements of prior materials on-time, as much as possible. Less prior materials are built up or delayed first, if necessary (of course they tend to be on-time as well).

Cost-Based Calculation

On top of master data, defining constraints, procurement options, and relations; PPO is modeled via costs and penalties to process existing transactional data and create receipts (as planned orders, stock transfer requisitions, and/or purchase requisitions).

"Priorities" mentioned above, are defined by costs and penalties. Higher the cost/penalty higher the priority.

Unlike MRP, PPO is not a heuristic; so, it does not have a specific sequence of tasks carried out defined by ABAP codes. It is an operational research tool, that builds a highly complex simplex method, running linear programming. That means it does not process something first then does the next thing, but all results are output of a huge single calculation. In doing so, for example, if "non-delivery penalty" is 0 (zero), then PPO may not create any receipt.

Or let's say a semi-finished product is overproduced or a raw material is over procured (probably due to defined lot sizes), then some stock will be left on hand for that component. If the stock cost is relatively higher then upstream stock cost + upstream production cost, then finished good receipts will be created as long as there is enough capacity, even tough there is no requirement for it, just to consume the component and not to keep it in stock.

Make-Buy Decision

In MRP, "procurement type" of a product determines whether it is produced or transferred or purchased, and it is a rigid rule. Even if quota arrangement is defined, MRP distributes the quantities according to quota definition. MRP does not consider all options, other than certain rules given.

However, based on constraints and source of supply costs, PPO can select from several source of supplies.

When to Use PPO?

All above mentioned way of working indicates that PPO is not easy to evaluate. It can never be verified (since it processes beyond human calculation and considers beyond human recognition) but can just be evaluated to be meaningful or not. So, it should be utilized if it is really required. Then how can we be sure that we can benefit from it? At this point there are some arguments to consider:

  • PPO does not replace MRP. PPO is a short and mid-term planning tool. After PPO horizon, MRP is still required. In order to segregate PPO and MRP, I recommend using fixing intervals on relevant resources.
  • PPO is bucket oriented, so it is suitable for periodical plans.
  • Although it is technically possible, it is not recommended to utilize PPO when production duration may exceed PPO buckets. If a planned/production order duration can be limited by a maximum lot size, and can have separate orders for separate buckets, then it will be better. Otherwise, it will be hard to manage orders and bucket capacities.
  • Although it is technically possible, personally, I don't recommend utilizing setup matrix durations. Instead, I recommend defining setup matrix based on setup costs, if required. Otherwise, the results may be inconsistent, because PPO does not actually sequence orders, so is not aware of actual setup times. To model capacity loss due to setup times, I personally prefer to define an average fixed setup duration.
  • As for all optimization problems, the results are not totally good or totally bad, but it has a quality. The output quality depends on complexity of the problem solved, so input scope. Therefore; keep the horizon and product/resource scope as less as possible to get better results. From purely performance point of view, there are workaround solutions to consider before descoping, please refer to 2965860 - Info on Performance of the Production Planning Optimizer - SAP for Me.
  • PPO has restrictions to apply. For example, it does not support below conditions:
    • Make-to-order scenarios
    • Engineered-to-order scenarios (WBS Element usage)
    • Characteristic Dependent Planning (CDP)
    • Subcontracting (at least for now)
    • Usage of schedule agreements
    • Resource networks
    • etc. (for all restrictions please refer to 3226003 - Restrictions and Implementation Recommendations for Production Planning Optimizer (SAP S/4HANA 2022) - SAP for Me)
  • PPO is an advanced tool, since it requires many restrictions and recommendations to apply. So, it requires an advanced level master data team and production planning team. For examples to consider, please refer to 3088785 - Production Planning Optimizer:FAQ - SAP for Me.

 

Spoiler
There has already been a lot to digest. We should conclude this blog now. Then we can continue with cost modeling, customizing, master data settings, PPO execution and result evaluation in later blogs; which I will share links below. Take care.

entry.title: Re: Do we have a standard CDS view to create an external...
entry.content: Hi, such a CDS view does not exist and has not been published by SAP. Best, Edrilan Berisha

if you are looking for a roadmap, please explicitly also say so. I cannot really understand everything from your question.

But my recommendation is to use SLG1 if you search for a specific log.

 

Best,

entry.title: Re: how to remove decimal places from field type number i...
entry.content: Yeah, you right its not suported in job requisition, this is probably a system limitation as of the moment and would suggest to raise an enhancment request. if no workaround is available.

entry.title: SCMW Error DLV_BATCG (107) when using Stock Segments in EWM
entry.content:
What is happening:

During the processing of the inbound delivery the following error occurs:

Details of the error message:

Explanation and how to proceed:

EWM gets the batch data from the class SAP_EWM_LOBM, which consists of reference characteristics to the batch fields in table MCH1. The reference to the stock segment field MCH1-SGT_SCAT is missing.
This can easily be fixed in two easy steps:

First, create a characteristic ZLOBM_SGT_SCAT in transaction code CT04.

In a second step assign the new characteristic to the class SAP_EWM_LOBM. Since SAP_EWM_LOBM classifies batches, the Class Type is 023.
Use transaction code CL02.

entry.title: [Webinar] Achieving Success with Real-Time Health Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM(Japanese)
entry.content:
SAP Cloud ALM Technical and Configuration Deep Dive 2025 

We are thrilled to extend a warm welcome to everyone for our SAP Cloud ALM technical and configuration deep dive webinar series.

Topic

Achieving Success with Real-Time Health Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM(Japanese)

Agenda

  • SAP Cloud ALM Overview - 5 min
  • Achieving Success with Real-Time Health Monitoring in SAP Cloud ALM - 50 mins
  • Q&A - 5 mins

Register Now

We look forward to seeing you virtually!

Stay updated on upcoming sessions!

For any further queries please contact us on cloudalm@sap.com

 

If you have any questions about the session topic, please feel free to post them in the comments.

entry.title: Re: Enabling checkbox Delivery Completed for released Purchase Order
entry.content:
This requirement has been solved by my colleague. For anyone who's having the same requirement, here is the code.

METHOD if_ex_me_process_po_cust~fieldselection_item.

  DATA: lwa_header TYPE mepoheader.

  lwa_header = im_header->get_data( ).

  IF ( sy-tcode = 'ME29N' OR sy-tcode = 'ME22N' ) AND lwa_header-frgke = 'R'.
    DATA(ls_fieldselection) = VALUE mmpur_fs(
        metafield   = '76'   " ELIKZ - Delivery Completed Indicator
        fieldstatus = '+').
    INSERT ls_fieldselection INTO TABLE ch_fieldselection.
  ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.

The metafield value for ELIKZ is 76, by inserting it to ch_fieldselection table on this method it will activate the Deliv. Compl. checkbox.

I will mark this topic as solved. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Error in Customer Master Migration in Migrate your Data
entry.content: Hi Vijaykumar, thank you for the advice. I'm able to upload after using a different external number.

entry.title: Re: Bin sorting in EWM
entry.content:
Hi @Yellappa ,

"Because putaway happens in bin naming sequence or as per sorting with Crossline put away sorting." -> Please do not confuse bin name with bin sorting 

-> Bin ( Bin Name): This is the Physical Identification of Location/Precise/Exact point of Warehouse Location. 

-> bin sorting: Technically it is a number in system however in business understanding it is an activity (Picking, Putaway, Replenish, Stock taking...etc) which should perform as per sequence(number) which could be define as per Harehouse operations . Example: Warehouse has bins called WH-EX-01-01,WH-EX-01-02,WH-EX-01-03, in this case business is willing to fill/putaway the Product A stocks first in WH-EX-01-03, then WH-EX-01-01, then WH-EX-01-02 and however business is picking product A first WH-EX-01-02, then from WH-EX-01-01 then from WH-EX-01-03, in this cases bin sorting must be used to cater such requirement. 

Hope it clears. 

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Enhancing Maintenance Order and Task List Structure Lists with additional field
entry.content:
We have a requirement to enhance both the Change Maintenance Order (W0017) and Change Maintenance Task List (W0021) apps' Materials Structure lists with an additional column displaying Material Type data. We have identified eams3_s_sp_struct_list as the underlying structure for the displayed list that will need to be extended, however it is currently restricted to be not extensible and therefore any changes would not be clean core compliant. Please confirm that altering the Structure Lists in this way is a product gap and please indicate when this structure will have a clean core compliant method of alteration.

  1. Open Fiori launchpad
  2. Navigate to either Change Maintenance Order (W0017) or Change Maintenance Task List (W0021)
  3. Open the "Operation Data" Tab
  4. Open the "Materials" Tab
  5. Open the "+ Select from Structure List" button
  6. Items are displayed, Material type, if relevant, is required to be displayed

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Enhancing Maintenance Order and Task List Structure L...

entry.title: Re: Planning Modernization
entry.content:
Thank you for reading!
I’d love to hear your perspectives on BW modernization—especially when it comes to how it’s shaping your cloud planning strategies.
Are you seeing similar shifts in your organization or projects? Do you think the cloud-native planning tools are mature enough to fully replace traditional BW-based approaches?
Let’s share insights and experiences—curious to learn how others are navigating this transition!
